title: ClickFix: Clipboard PowerShell Execution
description: Detects fake CAPTCHA / ClickFix social-engineering execution patterns where a browser-spawned process runs PowerShell with clipboard-themed instructions, download cradles, or hidden execution.
query: (('Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs') and 'Event ID' = '1' and ('Parent Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Parent Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Parent Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe') and 'Process Name' like '*\\powershell.exe' and ('Command Line' like '*ClickFix*' or 'Command Line' like '*fake CAPTCHA*' or 'Command Line' like '*clipboard*' or 'Command Line' like '*iwr*' or 'Command Line' like '*Invoke-WebRequest*')) or (('Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Event Security Logs') and 'Event ID' = '4688' and 'Process Name' like '*\\powershell.exe' and ('Command Line' like '*ClickFix*' or 'Command Line' like '*fake CAPTCHA*' or 'Command Line' like '*clipboard*' or 'Command Line' like '*iwr*')) | stats count as Hits, unique('Command Line') as Commands, unique('Parent Process Name') as Parents by 'Host Name (Server)', Entity, 'Process Name', 'Trace ID' | sort -Hits

ask_ai_prompts: Explain why this looks like ClickFix rather than normal PowerShell automation.
List the parent browser, command line, host, and trace ID for containment.

falsepositives: Helpdesk scripts launched from browser downloads
